existing_plant.heat_recovery_existing: wort cooler waste heat to brewing liquor (HLT) — partial

_golden_truth._note: Hand-calculated reference answers. Used by eval harness.
_golden_truth.baseline_carbon_assumptions: DEFRA 2026 natural gas factor 0.18293 kgCO2e/kWh; UK electricity factor 0.193 kgCO2e/kWh.

expected_shortlist_must_exclude_with_rea.biomass_boiler: particulate / brewing-product contamination risk on UK brewery sites
expected_shortlist_must_exclude_with_rea.100pc_hydrogen_boiler: no Tadcaster H2 network; reassess once HyNet expansion confirmed

balanced_pathway_sketch.year_1_2: MVR upgrade to wort kettle (recover ~15% of brewing steam)
balanced_pathway_sketch.year_2_3: 3 MW NH3 HP for HLT and CIP hot water, recovering wort + chiller waste heat
balanced_pathway_sketch.year_3_5: 5 MW electrode boiler for steam top-up under TOU tariffs + 12 MWh thermal store
balanced_pathway_sketch.year_5_plus: Full electrification once grid intensity <100 gCO2e/kWh and electricity:gas ratio normalises

key_decisions_for_senior_review: MVR retrofit on existing wort kettle has compatibility constraints — survey required
Steam quality at 10 barg may not be reachable by HP alone; electrode boiler hybrid is essential
TOU tariff arrangement with utility — material to electrode boiler economics
Brewing process integrity: any thermal recovery must not introduce off-flavours from heat exchanger residence time
Sensitivity: if 6 MW grid headroom not granted by DNO, balanced pathway delays 18 months
